mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive returns do not significantly exceed baseline during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or signal IC is negative over 2010-2020.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative forward returns in > 60% of triggered cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect supply-side shocks that disrupt corporate output and supply chains, causing negative equity returns, but this effect only materializes during periods of tight monetary policy.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, or produces positive returns in > 50% of tightening regime observations.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPI exceeds 3.0% year-over-year, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in > 55% of high-inflation observations, or signal IC remains below 0.01 across multiple inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Combined signal yields negative returns in > 60% of cases, or interaction effect is statistically insignificant in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP growth rates signal expanding economic activity and higher corporate profit margins, which systematically drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or positive returns do not significantly outperform baseline during GDP expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index exceeding 1.5% indicates accelerating manufacturing and utility output, which boosts equity valuations over the next week as earnings revisions turn positive.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or IC is negative during recovery phases.
